sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: North Texas’ Fluor Opens Office in Romania to Advance Next‑Gen Nuclear Energy Projects
feed.summary: Irving-based Fluor Corporation has opened a new European office in Bucharest, Romania, to strengthen its commitment to advancing nuclear energy projects in the region. This expansion supports Romania's goals for clean, reliable power and builds on Fluor's existing contributions to the country's nuclear development, including the SMR project in Doicești and the Cernavodă nuclear power plant expansion. The new office will serve as a hub for talent and execution capabilities, leveraging Bucharest's strategic advantages and skilled workforce.

feed.title: Cushman & Wakefield Plc Experiences Revision in Stock Evaluation Amid Market Fluctuations
feed.summary: Cushman & Wakefield Plc has seen an updated stock evaluation due to changing market conditions. The company's stock performance shows mixed technical signals and varied returns, outperforming the S&P 500 over the last year but struggling year-to-date. This revision highlights the complexities of its market position within broader economic trends.

feed.title: Direxion Daily TSLA Bull 2X Shares (NASDAQ:TSLL) Plans Quarterly Dividend of $0.10
feed.summary: Direxion Daily TSLA Bull 2X Shares (TSLL) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.0994 per share, with a record and ex-dividend date of March 24th and a pay date of March 31st. This dividend translates to an annualized yield of approximately 3.1%. The ETF, which provides 1.5x leveraged exposure to Tesla stock, traded up 0.4% at $13.00, with a market cap of $4.43 billion.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Modiv Inc. misses Q4 2025 earnings, stock stable
feed.summary: Modiv Inc. reported a significant miss for its Q4 2025 earnings, with EPS at $0.02 against a forecast of $0.07 and revenue of $11.07 million, missing expectations of $11.43 million. Despite the miss, the stock remained stable in aftermarket trading, rising 0.42% to $14.24, possibly due to its negative beta and strategic long-term initiatives. The company attributes the financial performance to lease expirations and asset sales, while maintaining a cautious outlook and a strategic focus on optimizing its industrial portfolio.

feed.title: Barclays Maintains Overweight on INCY Incyte Corporation March 2026
feed.summary: Barclays has reiterated an Overweight rating for Incyte Corporation (INCY) on March 24, 2026, despite mixed Phase 3 clinical trial results, indicating confidence in the company's commercial and pipeline longevity. Conversely, UBS lowered its price target for INCY to $94 while maintaining a Neutral stance, citing revenue concerns. Meyka AI gives INCY a B+ grade, reflecting its relative strength but also ongoing execution and trial risks, advising investors to use analyst ratings as a guide rather than a definitive buy/sell signal.

feed.title: Synopsys Brings Agentic Engineering Into Focus as AI-Driven Design Tests Productivity Limits
feed.summary: Synopsys is championing "agentic engineering" to significantly accelerate chip design through AI-driven simulation and generative design, aiming to reduce development cycles from years to months. While AI leads to substantial productivity gains, particularly in simulation and design iteration, these advancements come with high compute costs and a need for rigorous verification in highly automated systems. Despite increased automation, Synopsys emphasizes that AI will augment, not replace, human engineering judgment and accountability.

feed.title: Bio-Techne Advances Spatial Biology with Modular Expansion of COMET Suite
feed.summary: Bio-Techne Corporation has expanded its COMET spatial biology solution portfolio with new SPYRE Focus Panels and SPYRE Amplification Kits. These additions are designed to provide greater flexibility, higher sensitivity, and deeper insights for researchers studying complex tissue samples. The enhancements also include new features for HORIZON Image Analysis Software, further streamlining spatial biology workflows.

feed.title: Textron Inc. stock faces uncertainty amid aerospace delays and defense budget shifts in 2026
feed.summary: Textron Inc. is navigating challenges due to production delays in its aviation segment and intensifying U.S. defense budget deliberations for fiscal year 2026. While the company boasts a strong order backlog, particularly in its Bell helicopters and Cessna aircraft divisions, investors are cautious about near-term revenue recognition risks and potential adjustments to defense programs like the Future Vertical Lift. The industrial segment provides stability, but the overall sentiment is one of balancing opportunity with significant execution hurdles related to supply chain issues and government spending.

feed.title: HPE Stock Today: March 25 – £920m Mike Lynch Damages Awarded
feed.summary: A UK High Court has ordered Mike Lynch's estate to pay approximately £920m in damages related to HP's 2011 Autonomy acquisition, a decision without appeal permission. This ruling could significantly impact Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) and HP Inc financially, with proceeds potentially shared between the two entities following HP's 2015 split. Investors should monitor HPE's upcoming earnings call on June 2, 2026, for updates on the recovery's timing, accounting treatment, and its effect on capital allocation, while also considering the impact of GBP/USD exchange rates.

feed.title: News | CoStar World News for March 26
feed.summary: This CoStar World News report for March 26 covers several significant real estate transactions and trends globally. Key stories include SC Capital Partners acquiring Fusion Hotel Group in Vietnam, Landsec putting a famous London site up for sale for £450 million, and a joint venture targeting luxury hotels in Europe and North America. Additional news involves a Canadian pension fund entering the German residential market, a Montreal landmark office complex changing ownership, and a surge in demand for medical outpatient clinics in the Sun Belt.

feed.title: Ventas Inc. stock faces renewed pressure amid healthcare REIT sector volatility and rising interest
feed.summary: Ventas Inc. (ISIN: US92276F1003), a leading healthcare-focused REIT, is experiencing renewed pressure due to rising interest rates and fluctuating occupancy in senior housing and medical facilities. Despite its diversified portfolio of over 1,200 properties, the company faces challenges from elevated financing costs and tenant credit risks. US investors should monitor portfolio quality and dividend sustainability, as Ventas's long-term prospects are tied to demographic trends and its ability to manage debt and market volatility.

feed.title: Aetna, Elevance, Humana Medicare marketing lawsuit moves forward
feed.summary: A federal judge has allowed a lawsuit against Aetna, Elevance, and Humana to proceed, alleging that these Medicare Advantage insurers paid kickbacks to online brokerages. The lawsuit claims the kickbacks were intended to incentivize the enrollment of healthy Medicare beneficiaries. The article notes that this development was reported on March 25, 2026.

feed.title: KLA Corporation stock faces pressure amid semiconductor inspection demand slowdown signals in Q1 202
feed.summary: KLA Corporation's stock (KLAC) dropped 1.44% to $1,543.68 on Nasdaq, reflecting investor caution over potential softening demand for semiconductor inspection tools amid adjusting AI chip production cycles. Despite a strong year-to-date performance, the dip highlights KLA's sensitivity to market rotations and hyperscaler spending shifts. The company, a leader in wafer inspection, faces risks from inventory overhangs and geopolitical tensions, alongside potential benefits from AI-driven demand and sustained service revenue.

feed.title: DTE appeals $100 million federal judgment over Zug Island air pollution
feed.summary: DTE Energy is appealing a $100 million federal court judgment concerning sulfur dioxide pollution from its Zug Island coke facility. The court ruled that DTE violated the Clean Air Act by making major upgrades without proper permits, resulting in increased pollution. Besides the fine, DTE is required to fund $20 million in community air quality programs over seven years.

feed.title: Intercontinental Exchange : NYSE to Commence Delisting Proceedings Against FiscalNote Holdings, Inc. (NOTE)
feed.summary: The NYSE Regulation has initiated delisting proceedings against FiscalNote Holdings, Inc. (NOTE) for failing to maintain an average closing price of at least $1.00 over a 30 consecutive trading-day period, following a reverse stock split. Trading in the company's Class A common stock and warrants will be immediately suspended. FiscalNote Holdings has the right to appeal this decision before the Securities and Exchange Commission formally delists its securities.

feed.title: Hormel Foods' 2026 Guidance Signals Steady Recovery for Key Shelf-Stable Products Amid Analyst Adjus
feed.summary: Hormel Foods has released updated 2026 guidance, emphasizing strength in its foodservice and international segments to counteract challenges in retail for brands like Spam and Skippy. While analysts have slightly trimmed price targets, they remain optimistic about the long-term value of the company's stable consumer staples and its high dividend yield. The company's focus on operational efficiencies, product innovation, and strategic agility in higher-margin segments is key to its projected revenue stability.

feed.title: Steel Dynamics Inc. stock gains spotlight with KeyBanc overweight rating and $190 target amid steel
feed.summary: KeyBanc has initiated overweight coverage on Steel Dynamics Inc. (STLD) with a $190 price target, driven by the analyst's confidence in the steelmaker's resilient operations and its ability to navigate commodity cycles. The company's mini-mill model, disciplined capacity management, and consistent dividend policy are highlighted as key strengths, positioning STLD for outperformance in a recovering steel market. This bullish outlook aligns with broader industrial sector optimism and demand from infrastructure spending in the US.

feed.title: Sempra Is Up 8% Year to Date. Here’s Where the Stock Could Be Headed in 2026
feed.summary: Sempra (SRE) stock is up 8% year-to-date, driven by increased institutional investment due to rising electricity demand from data centers and industrial electrification, alongside its exposure to regulated utilities and liquefied natural gas (LNG) export projects. The company's $65 billion capital plan, focused on expanding its regulated utility rate base and LNG export capacity, is expected to provide steady long-term performance. While revenue growth is modest due to regulated markets, earnings are projected to grow faster as new projects come online, making Sempra moderately undervalued with future upside in infrastructure investment and LNG expansion.

feed.title: Kimbell Royalty (NYSE: KRP) controller sells 6,609 common units in market
feed.summary: Kimbell Royalty Partners' Controller, Blayne Rhynsburger, sold 6,609 common units at $14.48 per unit on March 23, 2026, as reported in a Form 4 filing. After this transaction, Rhynsburger retains a direct holding of 75,163 common units, indicating a continued significant position in the company. The filing notes the transaction as an open-market sale with a neutral impact and negative sentiment, despite his substantial remaining shares.

feed.title: Diana Shipping (NYSE: DSX) Co-CFO discloses common and Series B preferred stakes
feed.summary: Diana Shipping Inc.'s Co-CFO and Treasurer, Maria Dede, filed an amended Form 3, disclosing her equity holdings in the company. She directly owns 1,412 shares of common stock and 3,681 Series B Preferred Shares. The Form 3/A was an amendment to include the Series B Preferred Shares that were inadvertently omitted from the original filing.

feed.title: Neuronetics (STIM) ex-officer sells 46,289 shares to cover tax withholding
feed.summary: A former officer and director of Neuronetics Inc. (STIM), Keith J. Sullivan, sold 46,289 shares of common stock at a weighted average price of $1.34 per share. This non-discretionary sale was executed solely to cover tax withholding obligations arising from the vesting of a performance restricted stock unit award. Following the transaction, Sullivan still directly holds 1,590,924 shares of Neuronetics common stock.

feed.title: Haleon plc stock faces consumer health sector headwinds amid global economic pressures and parent co
feed.summary: Haleon plc, a consumer healthcare company spun off from GSK, is navigating global economic pressures and shifting demand patterns while demonstrating resilience. The company relies on its strong portfolio of 21 powerbrands like Sensodyne and Panadol, which contribute to its robust financial health and consistent growth. Despite risks from inflation, competition, and supply chain disruptions, Haleon offers a defensive play for investors due to its essential health products and strategic focus on innovation and cost efficiencies.

feed.title: PayPal Adds Alyssa Henry to Board of Directors
feed.summary: PayPal has appointed Alyssa Henry, former CEO of Block's Square business, to its Board of Directors, expanding the board to 12 members. This move aims to leverage her payments and technology expertise to bolster PayPal's role in the global payments market. Additionally, long-serving director Gail J. McGovern will retire after May 2025, with Ann Sarnoff taking over as chair of the Corporate Governance and Nominating Committee.

feed.title: PepsiCo Inc. stock faces pressure amid shifting consumer trends and competitive landscape in 2026
feed.summary: PepsiCo Inc. (US7134481081) is navigating challenges in 2026 due to shifting consumer preferences towards healthier, low-sugar options, which are impacting traditional soda volumes. While the snack division, particularly Frito-Lay, provides stability and strong margins, the company is focused on strategic responses, including innovation in functional beverages and cost discipline, to maintain its market position against competitors like Coca-Cola. US investors are watching PepsiCo's adaptation strategies, financial resilience, and strong dividend history as it faces regulatory scrutiny and inflation pressures.

feed.title: KKR Offloads CoolIT Systems For $4.75B To Ecolab – Know The Details
feed.summary: KKR has announced the sale of CoolIT Systems, a liquid data center cooling company, to Ecolab for $4.75 billion. This transaction is expected to generate approximately 15 times KKR's original equity investment. All CoolIT employees, who became owners through a broad-based ownership program, will receive substantial cash payouts ranging from one to over eight years of annual pay.

feed.title: Jack Henry & Associates stock faces pressure from regulatory scrutiny and macro challenges in community banking sector
feed.summary: Jack Henry & Associates (JKHY) stock is under pressure due to increased regulatory scrutiny on financial technology providers and macroeconomic headwinds impacting its primary clients, community banks. The company, a key software vendor for over 2,400 US community banks and credit unions, faces rising compliance costs and reduced tech spending from clients impacted by high interest rates and deposit shifts. Despite over 90% recurring revenue, investors should monitor these challenges, though the stock's defensive qualities and potential for growth in digital banking present a complex outlook.

feed.title: Paychex Is Executing on Its Paycor Acquisition
feed.summary: Paychex, a leader in payroll and human capital management software for small and medium-sized businesses, is reportedly executing effectively on its Paycor acquisition. The company's 2026 guidance has been maintained, indicating business as usual. Analyst notes also mention updating moat ratings and fair value estimates for payroll firms.

feed.title: Hesai 2025 Revenue Exceeds 3 Billion Yuan, Aiming for 3 Million Shipments This Year
feed.summary: Hesai Technology reported record financial results for 2025, with revenue exceeding 3 billion yuan and achieving GAAP profit for the first time for a lidar maker. The company delivered 1.62 million lidar units, a 222.9% increase year-over-year, driven by strong growth in ADAS and robotics sectors. Hesai anticipates doubling its shipments in 2026 to between 3 million and 3.5 million units, fueled by new design wins and strategic partnerships.

feed.title: Medtronic plc stock under pressure after cutting fiscal 2026 guidance on $157 million charge
feed.summary: Medtronic plc has cut its fiscal 2026 EPS guidance due to a $157 million one-time charge in its diabetes segment, specifically related to the MiniMed Flex product line, causing its stock to fall. The company lowered its non-GAAP EPS outlook to $5.50-$5.54 from $5.62-$5.66. This highlights vulnerabilities for US investors given Medtronic's significant North American revenue exposure and ongoing regulatory and competitive challenges in the medtech industry.

feed.title: Kymera Therapeutics COO sells $9m in shares
feed.summary: Kymera Therapeutics' Chief Operating Officer, Jeremy G. Chadwick, sold over $9 million in company stock through a pre-arranged trading plan. Concurrently, he exercised options to acquire additional shares. This comes as the stock shows significant annual growth but is down from its 52-week high, with InvestingPro indicating it may be overvalued.

feed.title: Teradyne Inc. stock faces AI slowdown pressures amid robotics expansion in 2026
feed.summary: Teradyne Inc. (TER) is experiencing stock pressure in early 2026 due to a moderation in AI-driven semiconductor demand. The company is strategically diversifying into robotics through its Universal Robots and MiR subsidiaries to mitigate risks from the cyclical semiconductor market. Despite sector headwinds, institutional investors demonstrate sustained interest, viewing Teradyne's blend of testing expertise and robotics as a strong bet on industrial automation and a defensive play in the semiconductor space.

feed.title: Illinois Tool Works stock draws attention amid institutional moves, dividend hike and automotive sector spotlight
feed.summary: Illinois Tool Works (ITW) is gaining investor attention due to mixed institutional activity, a recently declared quarterly dividend of $1.61 per share, and its recognition as a promising automotive stock by MarketBeat. The company, trading around $265 on the NYSE, reported strong Q4 results and robust FY2026 guidance, showcasing operational excellence and a commitment to shareholder returns despite some insider selling and a "Reduce" consensus rating from analysts.

feed.title: Palo Alto Networks: AI Threat Is Real (NASDAQ:PANW)
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks (PANW) stock is facing valuation pressure due to increasing AI-driven competition and slowing organic growth, despite its platformization efforts. Recent large acquisitions by PANW suggest gaps in its strategy and carry integration risks, while organic revenue growth decelerates. The article assigns a $120 valuation target, reflecting the impact of AI risks on its premium multiples.

feed.title: Kenvue stock faces acquisition uncertainty as SG Americas boosts stake amid pending Kimberly-Clark d
feed.summary: Kenvue (NYSE:KVUE) faces acquisition uncertainty as SG Americas Securities LLC recently boosted its stake in the consumer health giant by nearly 3 million shares, totaling over 3 million shares worth about $53.1 million. This comes as Kenvue's acquisition by Kimberly-Clark, announced in November 2025, is pending closure in H2 2026. The stock, currently trading at $17.55, is 28% below its 52-week high, sparking interest among investors for potential value and arbitrage opportunities despite regulatory and integration risks.

feed.title: Jefferies initiates Citi stock with buy rating on transformation progress
feed.summary: Jefferies has initiated coverage on Citigroup Inc. (NYSE:C) with a buy rating and a price target of $135.00, citing an improving return profile driven by revenue growth, expense control, and rising capital returns. The financial firm believes Citi is emerging from a multiyear transformation and expects it to achieve a low-to-mid teens return on tangible common equity. InvestingPro data also suggests the stock is undervalued, with a Fair Value of $133.26 against its current trading price of $114.50.

feed.title: Micron Technology's Earnings Skyrocketed 771% Last Quarter. This Is a Key Reason Why Its Growth Was So Incredible
feed.summary: Micron Technology experienced a phenomenal 771% surge in earnings last quarter, driven by a near tripling of sales and significant expansion in gross margins. This growth is largely attributed to strong demand for storage and memory products, particularly due to artificial intelligence. However, the article cautions that future earnings growth could be challenging to maintain if rising prices for these products stabilize or decline, potentially impacting the company's currently high gross margins.

feed.title: Norfolk Southern Corp. stock faces mixed signals from institutional moves and analyst views amid 2026 capex cuts
feed.summary: Norfolk Southern Corp. is receiving mixed signals from institutional investors and analysts, with some buying into the company's operational strengths while others trim positions due to projected 2026 capital expenditure reductions. Despite an anticipated sharp decline in capex, the rail operator shows resilience in cash conversion and is focusing on efficiency. Analysts have a mixed stance, with a consensus target of $315, as the company navigates regulatory hurdles for potential mergers and addresses concerns about pricing power and competition.

feed.title: Lindblad Expeditions Elevates Chief Maritime Officer to Executive Role
feed.summary: Lindblad Expeditions has appointed Rear Admiral Keith Taylor (Ret.), their Chief Maritime Officer, as an executive officer, effective March 19, 2026. This promotion underscores the company's focus on strong maritime leadership, leveraging Taylor's extensive experience from the U.S. Coast Guard and major cruise brands. His compensation package is performance-linked, aligning his incentives with the company's operational results and shareholder interests, with careful attention paid to governance practices.

feed.title: Charles Schwab, Wayfair New Targets of Credit Card Patent Suits
feed.summary: Wayfair LLC and Charles Schwab & Co. are among the latest high-profile companies to face patent infringement lawsuits related to contactless credit card technology. Induction Devices LLC filed separate complaints against them, along with Truist Bank and Costco Wholesale Corp., alleging infringement of patents concerning circuits and memory for high-frequency identification technology. These new lawsuits add to 23 other similar cases Induction Devices has filed since August 2025 against major companies like Dick’s Sporting Goods Inc., Lowe’s Cos., and JPMorgan Chase.

feed.title: KraneShares CSI China Internet ETF Options Spot-On: On March 25th, 134.88K Contracts Were Traded, With 2.84 Million Open Interest
feed.summary: On March 25th, the KraneShares CSI China Internet ETF (KWEB.US) saw significant options trading activity, with a total volume of 134.88K contracts. Call options accounted for 81.34% of these transactions, indicating a predominantly bullish sentiment. The open interest for KWEB.US stood at approximately 2.84 million contracts, representing 83.99% of its 30-day average.

feed.title: Jabil SVP Schick sells $278k in company stock
feed.summary: Jabil Inc.'s SVP and CHRO, Gary K. Schick, sold 1,000 shares of company stock for a total of $278,332, reducing his direct ownership to 40,843 shares. This transaction follows Jabil's strong Q2 fiscal 2026 results, which saw revenue increase by 23% year-over-year and analysts raising price targets due to strong AI demand. The company also announced a $1.1 million donation to St. Petersburg College for advanced manufacturing training programs.

feed.title: Liveperson CEO Sabino sells $20k in LPSN stock
feed.summary: Liveperson CEO Anthony John Sabino sold 8,033 shares of LPSN common stock for approximately $20,082 on March 23, 2026, under a pre-arranged trading plan. The shares were sold near the stock's 52-week low, following an 82% decline over the past year. Despite the challenging performance, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is undervalued.

feed.title: Verisign (VRSN) director Armstrong buys $3979 in shares
feed.summary: Verisign (VRSN) Director Courtney D. Armstrong recently purchased 15.909 shares of common stock for $3,979, increasing The Armstrong Family Trust's holdings. This comes as Verisign trades near $251.73 and maintains strong gross profit margins, though InvestingPro views the stock as slightly overvalued. The company recently reported mixed Q4 2025 earnings, missing EPS forecasts but exceeding revenue expectations.

feed.title: Liquidia Corp CCO Moomaw sells $673k in shares
feed.summary: Liquidia Corp's Chief Commercial Officer, Scott Moomaw, sold 18,314 shares of company stock for $673,751 and exercised options to acquire 17,000 shares for $57,800. These transactions were executed under a pre-arranged 10b5-1 trading plan. The company's stock has seen a 126% return over the past year, and analysts predict profitability this year, with strong revenue growth and positive price target revisions from several firms.

feed.title: Small automated IFF (IFF) stock trades reported by director Mark J. Costa
feed.summary: INTERNATIONAL FLAVORS & FRAGRANCES INC director Mark J. Costa reported small, automatic trades in IFF common stock via a tax loss harvesting account. These transactions, involving 10 or 15 shares at a time, occurred throughout 2023 at the broker's sole discretion, without Costa's control or prior knowledge. Following these trades, which resulted in losses, Costa directly holds 463 IFF common shares.

feed.title: FIS Expands Real Time Clearing And Payments As Valuation Gap Persists
feed.summary: Fidelity National Information Services (FIS) has launched a new 24/7 real-time clearing solution for prediction markets and completed a major acquisition of a card issuer business, making payments its largest revenue contributor. These strategic moves position FIS to cater to the growing demand from large commercial banks for scalable, automated, and AI-driven payment solutions. Despite these advancements, the company's shares are assessed as undervalued, trading approximately 67.1% below an estimated fair value.

feed.title: Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan, Inc. Announce Extension of Second Open Season for Remaining Western Gateway Capacity
feed.summary: Phillips 66 and Kinder Morgan, Inc. have extended the second open season for their proposed Western Gateway Pipeline system until April 15. This extension offers prospective shippers additional time to evaluate the updated Transportation Services Agreement, including rates, and secure approvals for the remaining capacity on the pipeline. The initial open season in December saw significant shipper interest and commitments.

feed.title: Assessing Loma Negra (NYSE:LOMA) Valuation After Mixed Returns And A Lofty 75.9x P/E Ratio
feed.summary: Loma Negra (NYSE:LOMA) shows mixed recent stock performance, with short-term gains but weaker monthly returns, while long-term shareholder returns are strong. Despite a high Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio of 75.9x and an analyst target price significantly above its current trading price, the company's valuation appears stretched given recent earnings decline and thin net margins. A discounted cash flow (DCF) model also suggests the stock is overvalued, trading above its estimated future cash flow value.

feed.title: U Power Ltd Announces 10-For-1 Share Consolidation - SEC Filing
feed.summary: U Power Ltd has announced a 10-for-1 share consolidation, as detailed in an SEC filing. This information was reported by Refinitiv. The article provides a brief notice about this corporate action.

feed.title: U.S. Stock ETF Tracker | Countdown to the Century IPO! Space-related concepts surge across the board
feed.summary: US stock indexes closed higher, with technology stocks advancing. Space-related concept ETFs saw significant surges, with RKLB and ASTS double-long ETNs rising 20% due to positive developments in rocket launches and satellite internet. Gold and silver-related ETFs also recovered, with double-long silver and gold ETNs up 7%, amid news regarding Middle East tensions.
